Advanced UVEA Clinic Services in Kochi, Kerala

The UVEA Clinic at Giridhar Eye Institute, located in Kerala, is dedicated to diagnosing and treating conditions related to the uvea, a vital part of the eye that includes the iris, ciliary body, and choroid. These structures are crucial for maintaining eye health, controlling light entry, focusing the lens, and nourishing the retina. Disorders of the uvea, such as uveitis, can lead to significant vision problems if not treated promptly. At our UVEA Clinic, we combine advanced diagnostic tools with expert care, ensuring that every patient receives the best possible treatment tailored to their specific needs.

Utilizing advanced tools for precise diagnosis and effective uveal condition treatment.

Specialists deliver top care blending global expertise locally.

Delivering Excellence in Uvea Health

We understand that eye health is integral to overall well-being, and our UVEA Clinic reflects this commitment. Located in the heart of Kerala, our clinic is recognized for excellence in patient care, innovative treatment options, and a compassionate approach. We prioritize patient education, ensuring that you are fully informed about your condition and treatment options. Whether you’re dealing with a complex uveal disorder or seeking a second opinion, our clinic is equipped to provide the highest level of care. Trust in our expertise, and let us help you maintain the health of your eyes with the best medical care available.

Frequently Asked Questions

Find answers to common concerns about uveitis care, treatment, and recovery, helping you better understand your condition and next steps.

Can uveitis cause permanent vision loss?

Yes, if untreated, uveitis can lead to vision loss. Early diagnosis and treatment can prevent complications.

How long does uveitis treatment take?

Treatment duration varies, lasting from weeks to months, depending on severity and response to medications.

Is uveitis a chronic condition?

Uveitis can be acute or chronic. Regular follow-up with an eye specialist ensures timely management and monitoring.

Can uveitis recur after treatment?

Yes, uveitis can recur, which is why ongoing monitoring and follow-up appointments are important to manage flare-ups.

Will I need surgery for uveitis?

Most uveitis cases are managed with medication. Surgery is only considered if complications, like cataracts, arise.

Are steroid eye drops safe for uveitis?

Steroid drops are safe under a doctor’s supervision and are effective in reducing inflammation in the eye.

 

Can uveitis affect both eyes?

Yes, uveitis can affect one or both eyes, depending on the underlying cause and type of inflammation.

Will I have to take medication for life?

Not always. Medication is typically short-term, but long-term treatment may be required for chronic uveitis management.
